---
title: "GitHub"
toc: true
toc-location: right
---

::: callout-warning
## Minimum requirement for this course:

1. a **personal GitHub account**
2. a **GitHub Copilot** subscription (private or institutional).
3. **available premium request** ([chek here](https://github.com/settings/copilot/features))
:::


> **Why?** GitHub hosts your code repositories and enables collaboration with
  version control. It also provides access to **GitHub Copilot**, an AI pair
  programmer that suggests and completes code directly in your editor.

**What you need to do:**

1. [**Create a GitHub account**](https://github.com/signup) with your
   [**personal email**]{style="background-color: #c0392b; color: white; padding: 2px 4px;"} (not your WB email).

2. [**Join the WB GitHub organization**](https://worldbankgroup.service-now.com/wbg/en/join-github-organization-account-request?id=wbg_sc_catalog&sys_id=910e1739db1a54903c5960ab13961912&table=sc_cat_item&searchTerm=github) ([github.com/worldbank](https://github.com/worldbank)).
   — See [WB GitHub Enterprise Overview](https://github.com/worldbank/ospo/tree/main?tab=readme-ov-file#octocat-github-enterprise)

3. **Get GitHub Copilot access** — either [request it from WB](https://github.com/worldbank/ospo/tree/main?tab=readme-ov-file#-github-copilot) or [subscribe privately](https://github.com/features/copilot/plans).

4. Update your **Copilot premium request budget** (optional but recommended) — the WB plan includes 300 free premium requests/month (private subscription includes 500/1500). Beyond that, requests are blocked unless you set up a budget. With a private subscription, you can can [update your Copilot premium request budget](https://github.com/settings/billing/budgets) through GitHub billing settings, while with the WB plan you need to follow **[Enable premium request chargeback](https://github.com/worldbank/ospo/blob/main/docs/copilot/premium-request-chargeback.md)**.

Questions? Email [github@worldbank.org](mailto:github@worldbank.org).

## Premium request (limits and overuse)

GitHub Copilot's advanced features beyond in-line code copletion rely on
"**premium requests**" to AI models like GPT-5+ and Claude Sonette/Opus.
Each copilot subscription plan includes a certain number of free premium
requests per month (WB org plan includes 300, private plans include 500/1500).
**Once you reach that limit, you lose access to advanced AI features for the**
**rest of the month**.

**Check your current premium request usage** here:
[GitHub → Settings → Copilot](https://github.com/settings/copilot/features)
and scroll down to the "Usage" section.

To avoid hitting the limit, set up a budget for overuse ($0.04/request).

- private subscription: [update premium request budget](https://github.com/settings/billing/budgets) through GitHub billing settings.
- WB org plan: setup [premium request chargeback](https://github.com/worldbank/ospo/blob/main/docs/copilot/premium-request-chargeback.md).

## Access Levels

Different setup unlock different capabilities:

| Setup | Repositories | GitHub Copilot | WB Org Repos |
|-------|-------------|----------------|--------------|
| No GitHub account | Public only | ❌ | ❌ |
| Personal account | Personal repos | ⚠️ Limited, no Claude | ❌ |
| + **Private** Copilot Pro/Pro+ subscription | Personal repos | ✅ Full (500/1500 req/mo), Claude; ❌ No access to Copilot after 500/1500 requests / month | ❌ |
| + Premium requests budget | Personal repos | ✅ 500/1500 req/mo + overuse budget | ❌ |
| + WB Org member | Personal + WB repos | ⚠️ Basic only (No premium request) | ✅ |
| + WB Org + **Institutional WB** Copilot access | Personal + WB repos | ✅ Full (300 req/mo), Claude/GPT. Overuse budget | ✅ |
| + WB Org + **premium requests chargeback** | Personal + WB repos | ✅ 300 req/mo + overuse budget | ✅ |

## Setup guide

### Create a GitHub Account

Create an account at <https://github.com/signup>. Use your [**personal email address**]{style="background-color: #c0392b; color: white; padding: 2px 4px;"}, not your World Bank email.


### Join [github.com/worldbank](https://github.com/worldbank)

1. Submit a [Join GitHub Organization Account Request](https://worldbankgroup.service-now.com/wbg/en/join-github-organization-account-request?id=wbg_sc_catalog&sys_id=910e1739db1a54903c5960ab13961912&table=sc_cat_item&searchTerm=github) on eServices to join [github.com/worldbank](https://github.com/worldbank)

2. Accept the invitation email sent to your GitHub account email

3. Confirm membership by following the steps in the email
   - If you don't accept the invitation within 7 days it expires — resubmit through eServices.

4. See [WB GitHub Enterprise Overview](https://github.com/worldbank/ospo/tree/main?tab=readme-ov-file#octocat-github-enterprise)

::: callout-warning
**Inactivity policy.** Members who have not contributed to any WB GitHub repository for several months may be removed from the organisation automatically. If you lose access, resubmit the eServices request to rejoin. This does **not** affect your personal GitHub account or Copilot subscription.
:::


### Access GitHub Copilot

You can either use Github Copilot **from the WB Organization** or **subscribe privately**.

To access GitHub Copilot through the WB organization, you need to request access after joining the WB org. Go to [GitHub → Settings → Copilot](https://github.com/settings/copilot).

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Copilot settings

![Copilot settings](../../assets/img/gh-copilot-access-1.png)

### Request access

Scroll down to **"Get Copilot from an organization"** and click **Request access** from the worldbank organization.

![Request access button](../../assets/img/gh-copilot-access-2.png)

### Email WB GitHub team

Then send an email to [github@worldbank.org](mailto:github@worldbank.org) with:

- Subject: `Request GitHub Copilot Access`
- Body: your GitHub username + brief reason
- Copy your manager for approval

:::

::: callout-info

### GitHub Copilot with a private subscription

If you have a personal Copilot subscription, you can use it with your GitHub account regardless of WB org membership. See [GitHub Copilot Plans for individuals](https://github.com/features/copilot/plans)

Once you are granted access to Copilot through the WB org, your private subscription will be terminated and you will be refunded unused budget. You cannot easily switch to private subscription after joining the WB org.
:::

### Verify GitHub Copilot

Go to [GitHub → Settings → Copilot](https://github.com/settings/copilot) and check your usage statistics.

::: {.panel-tabset}

### **No access:**

![No Copilot access](../../assets/img/gh-copilot-access-3a.png)

### **Access granted:**

![Copilot access confirmed](../../assets/img/gh-copilot-access-4a.png)

:::

### Enable Premium Request Overuse

The WB plan includes 300 premium requests/month. This is sufficient but limited.

To check your usage, go to [GitHub → Settings → Copilot](https://github.com/settings/copilot/features) and scroll down to the "Usage" section.

![GH Copilot 100% use limit](../../assets/img/gh-max.png){width="600"}

When you reach 100% of your monthly premium request limit, you will lose access
to advanced LLMs (Claude / GPT 5.4) for the rest of the month. You can still
use basic Copilot features that rely on older models, but the quality and
capabilities will be significantly reduced. The limit resets at the beginning of
each month.

To enable budget for overuse **enable [Premium Request Chargeback Process](https://github.com/worldbank/ospo/blob/main/docs/copilot/premium-request-chargeback.md)**:

1. Get manager approval and a budget code.
2. Set a monthly spending limit.
3. Email [github@worldbank.org](mailto:github@worldbank.org) with your budget code (cc your manager)

With a private plan, you can set up overuse through [Billing > Budgets](https://github.com/settings/billing/budgets).
